name: gearmand
explanation: Gearman speaks a plain-text admin protocol over TCP on the well-known port 4730. The check uses `python3_gearman.GearmanAdminClient` against `host:port` and explicitly defaults to `127.0.0.1:4730` when either field is omitted (`_get_conf` warns and falls back). No authentication is configurable. The optional `tasks` allowlist only filters per-task metrics; aggregate metrics work without it. A discovery probe can connect to TCP/4730 and issue an admin command (e.g. `version\r\n`, `status\r\n`) to confirm the server.
notes: Pattern C (TCP banner / known protocol). No existing auto_conf.yaml on disk, but the field set (host + port with universal defaults, no auth) is textbook generic.
